title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to hear people talking in eight situations. For questions 1–8, choose the best answer (A, B or C).
instructions: You will hear each piece twice. For questions 1–8, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear two people talking at a party.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: I don’t think we’ve met before. Are you one of Mark’s neighbours? ||Man: I used to be. I lived in Manchester when we first met at college, then moved to London for work. ||Woman: So you’re visiting for the party? ||Man: Not exactly. [1]I moved to Birmingham last month, and Mark works just round the corner from my new flat[/1]. He invited me tonight.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I don’t think we’ve met before. Are you one of Mark’s neighbours?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I used to be. I lived in Manchester when we first met at college, then moved to London for work.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So you’re visiting for the party?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not exactly. I moved to Birmingham last month, and Mark works just round the corner from my new flat. He invited me tonight.

questions.question: Where does the man live now?
questions.explanation: He states that he moved to Birmingham last month (evidence [1]).

extracts.context: You hear an interview on the radio with a cyclist.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: You’ve cycled through several European countries this summer, Nina. Where have you most recently been? ||Woman: I began in Belgium and crossed into France, which was harder because of the hills. After that I spent a week visiting friends in Spain. [2]I’ve only just returned from Portugal, where the coastal roads were fantastic[/2]. Switzerland is my next plan, perhaps next year.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: You’ve cycled through several European countries this summer, Nina. Where have you most recently been?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I began in Belgium and crossed into France, which was harder because of the hills. After that I spent a week visiting friends in Spain. I’ve only just returned from Portugal, where the coastal roads were fantastic. Switzerland is my next plan, perhaps next year.

questions.question: Which country has the cyclist most recently cycled through?
questions.explanation: She says she has only just returned from Portugal, distinguishing it from earlier and future trips (evidence [2]).

extracts.context: You hear a guide talking to visitors in an art gallery.
extracts.audio_script: Guide: I’m afraid the main picture gallery on the first floor is not open today. We had planned to move some of the paintings this week, but that work has been postponed. ||Guide: [3]During last night’s storm, water came through a window and damaged part of the wooden floor, so repairs are needed before visitors can enter[/3]. The sculpture gallery remains open as normal.

dialogue.speaker: guide
dialogue.text: I’m afraid the main picture gallery on the first floor is not open today. We had planned to move some of the paintings this week, but that work has been postponed.

dialogue.speaker: guide
dialogue.text: During last night’s storm, water came through a window and damaged part of the wooden floor, so repairs are needed before visitors can enter. The sculpture gallery remains open as normal.

questions.question: Why is the main picture gallery closed?

options.B: The room needs to be redecorated.
options.C: Part of the floor has been damaged.

questions.explanation: The guide says water from a storm damaged part of the wooden floor and repairs are required (evidence [3]).

extracts.context: You hear a man talking to a police officer.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Excuse me, officer. I’ve got a problem with my car. It’s the green one beside the bank. ||Officer: Has it broken down? ||Man: No, it’s running perfectly. I parked for five minutes to use the cash machine, and [4]when I shut the door I realised my keys were still inside, on the driver’s seat[/4]. My phone’s in there too, so I can’t call anyone.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Excuse me, officer. I’ve got a problem with my car. It’s the green one beside the bank.

dialogue.speaker: officer
dialogue.text: Has it broken down?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: No, it’s running perfectly. I parked for five minutes to use the cash machine, and when I shut the door I realised my keys were still inside, on the driver’s seat. My phone’s in there too, so I can’t call anyone.

questions.question: What is the man’s problem?

options.A: He has locked himself out of his car.
options.B: He cannot find where he parked his car.

questions.explanation: He says his keys are inside the car after he shut the door, meaning he is locked out (evidence [4]).

extracts.context: You hear a customer talking to a waiter in a café.
extracts.audio_script: Waiter: Would you like to order now? The soup of the day is tomato, and the sandwiches are listed over here. ||Woman: I had a large breakfast, so I don’t want anything heavy. I thought about a sandwich, but I’ll leave that. ||Waiter: We also have apple cake and a strawberry cream cake. ||Woman: [5]The strawberry one sounds lovely. I’ll just have a slice of that with coffee[/5].

dialogue.speaker: waiter
dialogue.text: Would you like to order now? The soup of the day is tomato, and the sandwiches are listed over here.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I had a large breakfast, so I don’t want anything heavy. I thought about a sandwich, but I’ll leave that.

dialogue.speaker: waiter
dialogue.text: We also have apple cake and a strawberry cream cake.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The strawberry one sounds lovely. I’ll just have a slice of that with coffee.

questions.question: What does the woman order?
questions.explanation: She chooses a slice of the strawberry cream cake with coffee (evidence [5]).

extracts.context: You hear a student leaving a message on an answering machine.
extracts.audio_script: Boy: Hi, Mum, it’s Liam. My first exam is over now. I was worried because it started at eight thirty, which is earlier than I’m used to, but I got there in plenty of time. ||Boy: [6]I knew the topics really well, and I managed to answer every question, so I’m feeling much better about the rest of the week[/6]. I’ll be home after lunch.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Hi, Mum, it’s Liam. My first exam is over now. I was worried because it started at eight thirty, which is earlier than I’m used to, but I got there in plenty of time.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I knew the topics really well, and I managed to answer every question, so I’m feeling much better about the rest of the week. I’ll be home after lunch.

questions.question: How does Liam feel about his exams?

options.A: He is confident after his first exam.
options.B: He is annoyed about an early start.
options.C: He is unsure what subjects to prepare.

questions.explanation: He says he knew the topics, answered every question and now feels better about the week (evidence [6]).

extracts.context: You hear a woman called Judy talking on the phone to her friend Martin.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Hi, Martin. It’s Judy. Are you still going to Anna’s party on Saturday? My sister was going to drive me there, but she has to work that evening. I know you said you were planning to get a taxi. ||Woman: [7]Would it be all right if I came in it with you? We could split the cost[/7]. I can walk to your house first if that’s easier. Call me back.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Hi, Martin. It’s Judy. Are you still going to Anna’s party on Saturday? My sister was going to drive me there, but she has to work that evening. I know you said you were planning to get a taxi.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Would it be all right if I came in it with you? We could split the cost. I can walk to your house first if that’s easier. Call me back.

questions.question: What does Judy want Martin to do?

options.A: give her a lift to a party

questions.explanation: Judy asks to travel in Martin’s taxi and share the cost (evidence [7]).

extracts.context: You hear a footballer talking about his career.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: You’ve had a long career, Dan. Is there one moment you’re proudest of? Winning the cup, perhaps? ||Man: Winning trophies is exciting, of course, and I once held the club record for appearances. But records get broken. [8]What means most to me is that, in nearly twenty years of matches, I was never sent off for bad behaviour[/8]. Young players should remember that respect matters.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: You’ve had a long career, Dan. Is there one moment you’re proudest of? Winning the cup, perhaps?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Winning trophies is exciting, of course, and I once held the club record for appearances. But records get broken. What means most to me is that, in nearly twenty years of matches, I was never sent off for bad behaviour. Young players should remember that respect matters.

questions.question: What is the footballer most proud of?

options.B: playing more games than anyone else
options.C: always behaving well on the pitch

questions.explanation: He says what means most is never having been sent off for bad behaviour (evidence [8]).
